title: derris praecox
intro: Derris praecox, also known as the poison nut or derris, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the legume family. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly due to the presence of rotenone, a toxic compound that has been used as an insecticide but also exhibits anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating various skin conditions, as well as demonstrating potential antimalarial and antibacterial effects. Derris praecox can be prepared into herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, although its use requires caution and should be under the guidance of a medical professional.
